# usageregistry_record Data Source

Reads one usage record by matching both target and consumer identifiers.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "usageregistry_record" "vault_secret" {
  target {
    type = "vault_secret"
    id   = "kv/secret-source/common/db"
  }

  consumer {
    type = "repository"
    id   = "https://git.projectbro.com/Devops/example-service"
  }
}

output "vault_secret_usage_action" {
  value = data.usageregistry_record.vault_secret.target.action
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `consumer` (Block, exactly one) Consumer being matched.
- `target` (Block, exactly one) Target resource being matched.

### Read-Only

- `annotations` (Map of String) Additional string annotations stored with the usage record.
- `created_at` (String) RFC3339 timestamp when the record was created.
- `id` (String) Stable usage record identifier.
- `updated_at` (String) RFC3339 timestamp when the record was last updated.
- `version` (Number) DynamoDB-backed record version.

### Nested Schema for `target`

Required:

- `id` (String) Provider-defined target identifier. Must not contain `#`.
- `type` (String) Target type name. Must not contain `#`.

Read-Only:

- `action` (String) Action performed against the target.
- `version` (String) Target-specific version, or `latest` when no version was provided.

### Nested Schema for `consumer`

Required:

- `id` (String) Provider-defined consumer identifier. Must not contain `#`.
- `type` (String) Consumer type name. Must not contain `#`.
